name: Aminophylline
executiveSummary: Increases diaphragmatic contractility. Apnea of the newborn.
indications:
## Main indications

Increases diaphragmatic contractility. Apnea of the newborn.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Loading 5–6 mg/kg slow IV; maintenance 0.5 mg/kg/h.

pediatrico.dose: IV loading dose: 7 mg/kg/dose over 20-30 minutes. Maintenance dose: 1-6 months: 0.4 mg/kg/hour, 6-12 months: 0.6 mg/kg/hour, 1-9 years: 0.8 mg/kg/hour, > 10 years and adults: 0.7 mg/kg/hour. Apnea of the newborn: loading dose: 5 mg/kg, maintenance: 5 mg/kg/day every 12 h (monitor serum levels to determine appropriate dose).
pediatrico.notes: Serum concentration: primary newborn apnea: 3-10 µg/mL, bronchodilator peak 10-15 µg/mL. Increase theophylline levels: erythromycin, ciprofloxacin, norfloxacin. Decrease theophylline levels: rifampicin, carbamazepine, phenobarbital, phenytoin. Increases risk of hypokalemia with high doses of salbutamol. For administration dilute with normal saline (preferably) or 5% dextrose to a concentration of 1 mg/mL (maximum concentration: 25 mg/mL).

neonatal.dose: Loading and maintenance according to NICU apnea protocol.

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Irritability, restlessness, seizures, headache, insomnia, palpitations, hypotension, extrasystoles, hyperglycemia, abdominal pain, vomiting.
